articles.title: US lawmaker says Congress has failed to assert its authority
articles.summary: US lawmaker says Congress has failed to assert its authority Progressive US lawmaker Ilhan Omar has criticised Congress for failing to challenge executive war powers and bring an end to the ongoing conflict with Iran. In a post on X, Congresswoman Omar said House Republicans withdrew a vote that could have restricted President Donald Trump's military actions because they believed it would pass and anger the president. She said that Congress had failed to exercise its constitutional authority over matters of war for 12 consecutive weeks and called for an immediate end to the conflict. Last

articles.title: UK maritime agency cautions vessels amid US military operations near Iran
articles.summary: UK maritime agency cautions vessels amid US military operations near Iran The UK Maritime Trade Operations (UKMTO) agency has warned that a military blockade on Iranian ports remains in force, restricting all inbound and outbound maritime traffic. In an advisory, the Royal Navy-linked agency urged merchant and neutral vessels transiting the Strait of Hormuz to remain at least 30 nautical miles (56km) away from US military units and to respond promptly to any radio communications from American forces to avoid being misidentified as a threat. articles.title: Hezbollah claims 22 attacks on Israeli forces in southern Lebanon
articles.summary: Hezbollah claims 22 attacks on Israeli forces in southern Lebanon The Lebanese armed group Hezbollah has claimed responsibility for 22 military operations carried out on Friday against Israeli forces operating in southern Lebanon. In a statement, Hezbollah said its fighters used rocket barrages, artillery fire, guided missiles, explosive devices and Ababil attack drones to target Israeli troops, vehicles and military infrastructure. articles.title: Pakistani officials hold talks with Rubio in Washington
articles.summary: Pakistani officials hold talks with Rubio in Washington Pakistani officials held what Islamabad described as a “warm and cordial” meeting with US Secretary of State Marco Rubio in Washington, DC, amid ongoing efforts to mediate between Washington and Tehran. According to Pakistan’s foreign ministry, Rubio acknowledged Pakistan’s diplomatic and mediation efforts during the discussions. Deputy Prime Minister and foreign minister Ishaq Dar said he remained confident that Pakistan’s continued efforts to promote peace and stability, supported by friendly countries, would produce positive outcomes.

articles.title: Wall Street Week | Britain’s Debt Problem, Poland’s Economic Boom
articles.summary: This week, the UK’s debt burden and weak growth are reviving fears that financial markets could once again destabilize British politics and policy. And, investors see data centers as long-term infrastructure, but neighbors worry about noise, water use, power demand and lasting costs. Plus, is Poland is one of the world’s fastest-growing economies, but it faces challenges to keep up its rapid rate of growth. Later, war in the Middle East and US tariffs are simultaneously tightening global aluminum supply and pushing prices sharply higher worldwide. (Source: Bloomberg)
